sources.notes: Dynamic grid problem with decreasing updates; maintain the number of equal-value components that are sinks.
Official editorial models each updated cell as a new time-version node and maintains equal-value components by DSU.

annotation.summary: Maintain the beauty of a grid under point decreases, where equal-value connected components are grouped and a component contributes if it has no strictly smaller neighbour.
annotation.constraints: The total number of cells plus updates is linear enough for near-O((nm+q) alpha(nm+q)) DSU with local neighbour updates.
annotation.core_idea: Treat every current value of a cell as a version node. Initially union adjacent equal-value nodes and mark whether each component is important, i.e. has no smaller neighbour. On a decrease, only the changed cell and its four-neighbour components can change contribution, so subtract their old contribution, create a new node for the new value, invalidate affected larger components, union equal-value neighbours, and add the new local contribution.
annotation.complexity: O((n m + q) alpha(n m + q) + q log 5) per test case.
tricks: create a new node for each value version
subtract and re-add only affected components

solution_variants.name: versioned-node-dsu
solution_variants.summary: Represent each cell update as a new DSU node, union equal-valued current neighbours, and maintain an important flag per component.
solution_variants.complexity: O((n m + q) alpha(n m + q))

tags.evidence: Equal-valued grid components are maintained with DSU unions and component-level flags.
Each decrease creates a fresh node for the new value of a cell instead of mutating the old DSU component.
tags.description: DSU modeling where updates create new version nodes for an object, while old nodes preserve historical components or invalidated states.
tags.created_reason: This is a reusable dynamic-connectivity modeling trick distinct from ordinary static DSU.

tags.evidence: The maintained objects are connected components in the 4-neighbour grid graph.
The answer is updated by removing and re-adding only components adjacent to the changed cell.
